item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the human(?) took off its flesh and only left the brain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Necropost, but I'm trying to leave constructive criticism on every skip I downvote. For me, this is missing the "conservation of weirdness" point. I love the brain in a jar aspects of this, and the tweets are great, but the angry triangles of doom make me downvote. They seem unrelated and uninteresting and serve no purpose. I'd recommend they either be given a purpose or be removed entirely.

-=Edit=-

So I stepped away from this for a minute and realized I missed something the first time. This thing is a reality bender, her wishes are granted in strange ways. She's a majestic brain in a jar because that's what she wanted. Some cute boy brought her the flowers (angry triangles) and she wishes that they show her the world, apparently they are trying to provided her with locomotion.

So it's a simple mind with simple wishes that are being granted in simple ways. You get my upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ^This. Although the bot idea doesn't sit well with me, because eventually, it would run out of new stuff to post and would repeat itself. Plus, I don't know about the average person following this account, but I might notice something different, something a bit off. Also, and I don't think that this would sit well with the Foundation, they should have kept the Twitter account up just a little longer so that they could develop an API to receive the messages that -A-1 is sending before they shut the Twitter account down. Because, you know, it's the Foundation, and they love to keep stuff around just for curiosity's sake, and a brain in a glass column tweeting 2 harmless things a day isn't that abnormal to the people who read the tweets, because as far as they're concerned, it's just a normal person on the other end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can understand investigating the followers, but giving amnestics to 21 people to help them forget a shitposting twitter account seems like total overkill. I think I'd rather see the Foundation replace the account with a bot (it would be easy, random weather and random desire posted once a day) and keep monitoring it for people who try to interact with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's just a bit too vague to not know what's going on here and it's irritating, but the writing and the tweets can help me slightly overlook this to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The tweets are good, but i'd like a better explanation of how they found it. It doesnt seem like the kind of thing that would be blatantly obvious

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Heh. It's not bad but it's not really good either. The thing is, it seems to me to be weird for weirdness' sake. I know this may be a strange criticism to make for anything SCP related, but what I mean by that is that the various strange elements don't seem to have any connection (other than the tetrahedrons trying to reach the column).

I like ambiguity but the thing is, ambiguity only works if you give the audience enough material to speculate or interpret your work, otherwise it's not ambiguous or even absurd, it's merely random and the reader has nothing to work with. And if they have nothing to work with, they have no reason to care.

It does have potential though, so I'm not voting either way for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I could have sworn I've seen this before, and commented on it. I think I downvoted because it makes no sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. Not, massively, but enough for a +1. It's not all that weird. It's not funny. It's not particularly scary, dangerous, or hard to contain. But it is fairly interesting, a bit dangerous, and reasonably weird. It feels like one of the weird things that would be sitting in the Foundation's facilities and be virtually unknown outside the relevant researchers . I think it earns a place in the setting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The tweets are adorable, but up until "internet", I could only think "Isn't this the -1 from the deer-god article I just read?" Just a case of unfortunate timing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very intriguing, I really like the tweets. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure what the "itchy jacket" refers to, but other than that, I like this. It's cute. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is very weird and confusing in a manner that I don't like. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm left hollow by this. I'm not sure what I'm reading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like I said in IRC, the tweets really make this article.
